title: Make TestStreamParser_RealClaudeWithToolUse deterministic or properly gated
description:
Pre-existing flake (confirmed twice during the v2 roadmap, baseline-identical): internal/agent/stream_integration_test.go TestStreamParser_RealClaudeWithToolUse invokes the real claude CLI; the model nondeterministically makes different tool choices, so assertions sometimes fail on a healthy system. Currently guarded by testing.Short() + claude-CLI-presence, so CI (-short) skips it — the flake hits local full runs.

First DIAGNOSE: read the test and the assertions that flake. Then pick the smallest fix that keeps real coverage, in preference order:
1. Loosen assertions to invariants that hold for ANY valid model response (e.g. stream parses, terminal event arrives, tool_use events well-formed if present) instead of asserting specific tool choices.
2. If invariant-only assertions would gut the test, add a recorded-fixture variant (capture one real stream output as testdata, parse it deterministically) and keep the live test behind an explicit env var (e.g. TICKS_LIVE_TESTS=1) on top of -short.
Do NOT simply delete the test or its live path — gate it.

Files: internal/agent/stream_integration_test.go (+ testdata/ if option 2).
Acceptance: go test ./internal/agent/ -run TestStreamParser_RealClaudeWithToolUse -count=5 passes 5/5 (or skips cleanly when gated); go test -short ./... still green.

Run: go test ./internal/agent/... && go vet ./...
acceptance_criteria: 5 consecutive runs pass or skip cleanly; -short suite green; live coverage preserved behind gate or invariant assertions
